items.title: Coalition Urges Policymakers to Reject No Surprises Act Enforcement Bill - MedCity News
items.reason: This item addresses federal No Surprises Act policy and potential legislative enforcement changes, which directly impacts NSA implementation and regulatory framework affecting revenue-cycle operations, though the excerpt lacks specific bill details needed for full operational relevance.
items.summary: A coalition has urged policymakers to reject a proposed No Surprises Act enforcement bill, signaling organized opposition to strengthened enforcement mechanisms under the federal law. The item reflects ongoing legislative and advocacy activity around NSA implementation, though specific details about the bill's provisions, timeline, and enforcement targets are not provided in the excerpt. This development is relevant to revenue-cycle teams as changes to NSA enforcement authority could affect balance-billing compliance requirements, Independent Dispute Resolution processes, and provider liability exposure. The coalition's opposition suggests potential shifts in how the law may be enforced and could influence future rulemaking or regulatory guidance from HHS/CMS.

items.title: CVS Caremark reaches settlement with FTC over insulin suit
items.reason: This reports an FTC settlement against a major PBM (CVS Caremark) involving regulatory enforcement and formulary practices that directly impact payer drug-coverage policies and provider reimbursement.
items.summary: CVS Caremark reached a settlement with the Federal Trade Commission over insulin pricing practices, announced Tuesday. The settlement requires the PBM to stop preferring higher-cost drug versions on standard formularies, mirroring terms imposed on Express Scripts in an earlier FTC action this year. This enforcement action against a major pharmacy benefit manager affects how payers structure drug coverage and pricing, which directly impacts provider reimbursement rates and patient access to medications, making it material for revenue-cycle teams monitoring payer policy constraints.

also.title: FTC settles with Caremark as lawsuit against ‘big three’ PBMs winds down - HealthExec

items.title: Medicare Advantage insurers face new bipartisan scrutiny over AI and care denials - STAT
items.reason: This item reports regulatory scrutiny and enforcement action by multiple government bodies (bipartisan congressional oversight) targeting Medicare Advantage insurers' practices around AI-driven care denials, which directly falls under CMS/Medicare Advantage sanctions and regulatory enforcement.
items.summary: Medicare Advantage insurers are facing new bipartisan congressional scrutiny regarding their use of artificial intelligence in claims denials and care authorization decisions. The oversight effort focuses on whether AI systems are inappropriately denying or delaying medically necessary care to beneficiaries, raising concerns about operational practices and fiduciary responsibility. This regulatory attention targets major MA carriers and their claims-denial processes, which directly impacts revenue-cycle operations through potential enforcement actions, policy changes, and claims review requirements that revenue teams must navigate.
